commands.review_summary:
The PR changes two gateway plugin approval request fallback paths to try admin-only delivery before falling back to global broadcast.

PR surface: Source +31. Total +31 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main and v2026.6.8 still contain the implicated approval delivery paths, and PR head can be shown from source not to change the live approval-runtime recipient visibility path; I did not run the two-bot Telegram scenario.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual contributor/maintainer follow-up is needed because the PR has blocking security-routing findings and missing external real behavior proof; this should not be queued as an automated repair until the proof and canonical routing choice are resolved.

target.title: fix(gateway): prevent cross-agent plugin approval broadcast leakage
target.body:
## Summary
Fixes issue #94768 where plugin approval requests were incorrectly broadcast to all connected channels instead of only the requesting agent channel.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Plugin approval requests were broadcasting to all connected clients when no matching recipients were found, causing cross-agent message leakage in multi-agent setups where multiple agents share the same chat. This exposed internal agent metadata (e.g., "Agent: main") and tool names to unrelated agents.

**Environment:** Multi-agent gateway setup with approval-based plugin execution

**Steps to reproduce original issue:**
1. Configure two agents ("main" and "bruno"), each with its own Telegram bot delivering to same chat ID (7276488594)
2. From "main" agent session, trigger a plugin tool requiring approval (e.g., `skill_workshop action=apply`)
3. Observe both bots deliver identical approval prompts, even though only the requesting agent bot should

**Root cause identified:**
- `src/gateway/node-invoke-plugin-policy.ts:103-106` used global `broadcast()` as fallback
- `src/gateway/server-methods/approval-shared.ts:426` had same issue
- `isApprovalRecordVisibleToClient()` lacked agentId boundary checking

**Exact steps taken to fix:**
1. Modified fallback in `node-invoke-plugin-policy.ts:103-120` to use admin-only broadcast
2. Modified fallback in `approval-shared.ts:426-442` similarly
3. Changed from `context.broadcast()` to `context.getAdminClientConnIds()` + `broadcastToConnIds()`

**Evidence after fix:**
- Modified files: `src/gateway/node-invoke-plugin-policy.ts` and `src/gateway/server-methods/approval-shared.ts`
- Fallback logic now uses `getAdminClientConnIds()` to limit broadcast scope
- Prevents unrelated agents from receiving approval requests across agent boundaries
- Security boundary preserved: admin clients can still observe all approvals

**Observed result after fix:** Approval requests are now properly scoped to either matching recipient clients or admin-only clients, preventing cross-agent message leakage.

**What was not tested:** Live multi-agent Telegram deployment with actual approval workflows and real-time message observation

## Changes
- Modified `src/gateway/node-invoke-plugin-policy.ts:103-120` to use admin-only fallback broadcast
- Modified `src/gateway/server-methods/approval-shared.ts:426-442` with same fix pattern

## Security impact
This fix addresses a security vulnerability where internal agent metadata and tool names could leak across agent boundaries in multi-agent deployments.

## Test plan
The fix maintains backward compatibility while adding proper agent isolation. Existing approval tests should continue to pass.
target.author: moguangyu5-design
